SkyTalk Docs
SkyTalkGetting Started
Architecture

Getting Started

How to run the full SkyTalk stack locally — server, database, mobile app, and admin portal.

This page covers running the stack on your local machine for development and testing.

Prerequisites

ToolVersionNotes
Node.js20+LTS
Docker24+Real Docker via get.docker.com — not snap
pnpm10.23.0Managed via Corepack
Android StudioLatestFor mobile emulator testing

Setup

Clone and Bootstrap

pnpm setup:local enables Corepack, pins pnpm to 10.23.0, installs all workspace dependencies, and runs Android environment checks.

Start the Server

Create packages/server/.env:

Then start Docker Compose:

Docker Compose starts Postgres 16 and the Node.js server. On first start, the server runs prisma db push to create all tables, then listens on port 3000.

Verify it's up:

Seed Development Data

Creates:

  • admin / admin — role: admin
  • pilot1 / test — role: user
  • Talkgroup: Ground Ops (both users as members)

Mobile App

The app defaults to mock mode — no server required. MockRelaySocket echoes all messages back locally.

To connect to the real server instead, create packages/mobile/.env.local:

Admin Portal

Opens at http://localhost:5173. Requires an admin JWT in localStorage. Get one:

Then in the browser console:

Environment Variables Reference

See Environment & Config → for the full table of all variables across all packages.

What Works in Mock Mode

FeatureMock ModeReal Mode
PTT audio record + playback✓ loopback✓ relay
AES-GCM encryptionpass-through (no real encryption)wired to KDF
Floor control arbitration✓ single-device✓ multi-device
Talkgroup membershiphardcodedfrom server REST
GPS signalsimulatedfrom DLS-140
Presencesimulatedfrom server WebSocket

Mock mode uses a pass-through crypto polyfill — audio is transmitted unencrypted. Never use mock mode on a real satellite link.
